name: Qelochia
short_description: On a secret arctic research base, a scientist, soldier, AI, and eldritch beast struggle for happiness. A short visual novel about love and pain set in an alternate history where humanity is ruled by eldritch gods.
detailed_description:
!!!

AN ISSUE OF UTMOST URGENCY REQUIRES YOUR ATTENTION. THERE HAS BEEN AN INCIDENT AT THE INGOLSTADT RESEARCH BASE, RELATED TO THE SHELLEY PROJECT. DECISION-PARALYSIS IS IN EFFECT. PLEASE REVIEW THE NECCESSARY CONTEXT, AND GRANT YOUR INPUT.

FEATURES

  • Comb through the logs of a secret arctic research base.

  • Learn about the lives and struggles of four unique inhabitants.

  • Experience a strange world where humanity lives in symbiosis with eldritch gods.

  • Make a critical decision.

CHARACTERS

Impiana Aneria

A researcher dedicated to cracking the secrets of her employer's enemy. Lives only for her work.

Frost

A Cybercarcass assigned to keep Impiana safe. A weapon craving belonging and use.

origin/management/research/arctic/ingolstadt/shelley/monitor

A distant branch of the superintelligence Origin, made to monitor the Ingolstadt base. Born with endless curiosity.

???

???

GAME INFO

Qelochia is a mostly linear visual novel, with roughly two hours of content.

reviews.review_text:
[h2]PLEASE give Qelochia a shot. I am BEGGING. [/h2]
It is [i]easily[/i] worth $5.00 and a few hours of your time. The game description and conceptual pitch simply do NOT give it justice — I promise you that whatever you're [i]expecting[/i] this game to be, you are not prepared for what this game [i]actually is[/i] — and once it sinks it's hooks in... you will be utterly enthralled until you see it through.
This is a difficult game to review. Going in blind, slowly learning about the characters, the setting, and where the plot was heading... was such a huge part of what enamoured me, but I can't [i]share[/i] any of that without SPOILING everything! So much of what makes Qelochia amazing is simply experiencing the narrative unfold for yourself.

[h3]But, okay, fine. Here's my best pitch: [/h3]
This is what I feel like I [i]can[/i] share that might help convince you to give this game a shot: 
[list]
[*]It's an excellently paced slowburn narrative with fantastic prose. It doesn't drag on or waste your time — every scene progresses the narrative and expands on the characters and the world they inhabit.
[*]Do you like worldbuilding? Do you like rich and expansive settings? Do you like when settings explore the sociopolitical implications of their fantastical elements? Do you like when this is all communicated organically, rather than in boring exposition dumps or random flavour text? If the answer is yes, then I have got great news for you...
[*] This game has Themes. They are significant and plentiful, and yet never feel heavy handed or out of place at all. From the grounded and painfully human, to the existential and metaphysical... boredom, crushes, hating your job, suffering, war, and what it means to have a purpose. 
[*] It's a love story. You get to choose how it ends. You won't want to. 
[/list]
[b]JUST. PLAY THIS GAME!!! OKAY? PLEASE!!? I'M GOING ACTUALLY INSANE!!![/b]
I usually don't play visual novel games, but this story was very entertaining and made me finish in one sitting

Qelochia comes at a time where my interest in writing-centric games is at its peak. And the kind of writing I enjoy the most gives you glimpses into a world that is stranger and more complex than you could assume from the start. It's clear that the developer has spent a lot of time thinking about this setting and the figures that inhabit it, and they've shown the perfect amount of restraint in conveying those thoughts to the player. Each new perspective captures another angle and deepens my fascination. This is the kind of worldbuilding that will leave me ruminating on theories late at night.

Now what about the actual game? Well, it's interactive fiction. A story to be taken scene-by-scene at your pace, organized through a good user interface with just the right amount ambience and feedback to satisfy without distracting from the writing. The developer knew not to bog down the story with gimmicks: there is only one choice and they made sure that it mattered. The characters are believable and painfully human, the visuals are a hypnotic set of pseudo-ASCII art and hand-drawn portraits that grease the wheels of imagination, and the conclusion is impactful. Worth five bucks and 2 hours, easy.

TL:DR - If you like the strange worldbuilding and writing of games like Sunless Sea and Cultist Simulator, or generally enjoy character-focused science fiction stories, Qelochia is worth your time.

An exploration of what it means to love, and how the ways in which we connect with our beloved is shaped by the systems and experiences we come from. The worldbuilding is intriguing and the characters are fun. I am team philonitor all the way. 

The sound design is good and sets the mood for what is happening, and the minimalist ascii background art is cool. I also liked the character portraits as well.

Qelochia is a short game about two parallel developing romances. It's long enough to leave you wanting more from the setting and characters, and I hope that the dev does decide to continue either the respective stories within Qelochia, or just within the setting itself. Definitely worth the money in my personal opinion.
